name: claude-toolkit
description: BeSpunky's Claude Code skills, subagents and commands. Develop once here; install and continuously upgrade across all projects.

plugins.name: project-starter
plugins.description: Scaffold new BeSpunky-standard projects: integrated Nx monorepo + Angular (clean --minimal app, no demo) + devcontainer (Claude CLI & VS Code extension) + tailored CLAUDE.md. Generator-first.

plugins.name: engineering
plugins.description: BeSpunky engineering skills. Agnostic foundation (architect-mentality + architecture-first: solve through design, never a patch; fix the root cause; design + confirm refactors) plus technique domains with progressive-disclosure references: software-design, advanced-typescript, angular-architecture, angular-native-wrappers, nx-monorepo-and-dx.

plugins.name: browser-automation
plugins.description: Drive a real browser via Playwright (Chromium, pre-installed in BeSpunky devcontainers) — verify UI behavior end-to-end, reproduce bugs in the running app, capture before/after screenshots, scrape rendered DOM, generate test scaffolds via codegen. Loaded by default in scaffolded projects.

plugins.name: product-ux
plugins.description: BeSpunky product & UX design skills. distill-the-brief: the upstream FRAMING stage of the experience-design quartet - turn a messy request or dossier (docs, research, a product concept, a vague one-liner, a long thread) into the correct INPUT for envision-the-experience: the design problem(s), at the right altitude, with the solution stripped out, before any world/feeling/pixel. The core move - distil the PROBLEM, never the solution: read past the pre-baked answers a dossier carries ('a soft door held for one breath', 'a dashboard', 'a three-step wizard') to the real ache/intent/feeling/requirements underneath, and hand envision the problem so it can imagine the form - because a brief that smuggles in the solution turns visioning into transcription (the generic, off-the-feeling failure). Finds the altitude (too LOW = a whole solution; too HIGH = a vibe with no problem), keeps only the LOAD-BEARING constraints (a concept's twenty commandments drown envision - carry the few that would make a design wrong), and decides the GRAIN - what is one design problem (one envision-able, screenshot-able moment) vs many - splitting a plural intake into surfaces along real seams AND modelling the englobing CONTEXT they inhabit (the app/world/module is itself a design problem - its spine, world-rules, and how the surfaces compose and the whole recedes/bridges - framed WITH its children in mind so the split never fragments into unrelated designs). Produces a Brief Tree - an englobing context brief plus the surface briefs native to it, each a clean, solution-free envision-input (the design problem - who it's for and the ache - the feeling - the functional requirements as needs - only the load-bearing constraints - what is explicitly out, including the form). The new upstream member of the quartet distill-the-brief -> envision-the-experience -> stage-the-vision -> realize-the-vision (problem -> feeling -> art -> build); an expression of architect-mentality (build for the goal not the brief; model the missing concept - the englobing context designed for its children; refuse false tradeoffs; concentrate complexity so the edges stay simple). keep-users-oriented: whenever you make someone wait or move them through a process, answer the three questions — what is the expected result, where am I, what is the next step — and pick the right feedback for deterministic (steps/progress) vs nondeterministic (estimate + notify) processes. A universal service-design principle, expressed primarily through software UI (loading/progress, async, multi-step flows, long-running jobs, notifications, optimistic UI). astonishing-to-use: the co-equal force to the experience-design trio — a design must be astonishing to USE, not only to look at. The trio optimizes look and feel (the art); this optimizes the USE (UX): effortless, understandable, free of hoops, respectful of attention, a joy in the hand, given how people really hold devices and feel using them (thumbs, one-handed, distracted, tired, bad signal). The use and the look are two co-equal forces that refine each other in a PING-PONG, never a one-way check (concept↔ux, however many rounds); the mission sets who leads (utility → UX leads; brand/art → concept leads then pressure-tested) but it is always a loop, and you never ship great beauty with mediocre use. Hard bar: never below great UX (architect-mentality: refuse false tradeoffs — beautiful AND usable). keep-users-oriented is one facet. Tenets: respond instantly and like real life (direct manipulation follows the finger), never hang the UX (heavy work async/background), load instantaneously (no/short-live splash, lazy + sized placeholders), smooth transitions everywhere, avoid abrupt layout shifts (CLS) at all costs, action -> fitting reaction, lowest-friction input controls, facilitate input of each data type (even inventing a new control the world hasn't seen — our genius art), different layouts per device (not reflow), plan for device realities (the mobile keyboard). When validation or anything fails, be informative (what/why/how-to-fix — never just 'invalid') and carry the user to the problem (scroll into view / take-me-there, focus the control). A router (friction & flow; facilitating input; clarity & cognitive load; errors & recovery; responsiveness & perceived speed; continuity & transitions; embodied & contextual use; reconciling art & use; joy of use). redesign-means-rethink: the anti-anchoring entry gate — when asked to REDESIGN a UI, treat it as a complete creative reconception FROM SCRATCH, never a modification or reskin of existing code; the existing implementation has ZERO design authority (do not read it to inform the new design), reconceive the form from intent/requirements/feeling while honoring the purpose (what it is for, its data and functionality), and read the old code only AFTER the new design exists to plan cleanup/overwrite/migration — then a redesign runs the trio from scratch. A targeted tweak is NOT a redesign. envision-the-experience: the upstream visioning move — imagine the world an interface lives in (its feeling, place, metaphor) before any layout or component, and design every element as a native inhabitant of that world; the screen is a place, not a page. Interrogate every element the spec names (a 'menu' might become a sunflower whose petals you pick), speak in sensory non-technical language that names no implementation, hold immersive back from busy (restraint over spectacle) — then LAND the world on the app: anchor every inhabitant to the app's real anatomy (surfaces, flows, gestures, states), validate every transformed element against what the convention it replaced was buying (findability, scannability, thumb reach, safety) and against general app truths (one thumb, interruptions, long lists, empty/loading/error states), and produce a directable Vision (five beats incl. the anchor — the world-to-app map a stakeholder can point at and steer). These three form the experience-design trio (feeling → Staging → build), on one altitude ladder: sensory feeling → web-native art → engineering. stage-the-vision: the visual architect and creative MIDDLE — the Vision says what a screen should FEEL like but not what it should LOOK like, and grounding alone can't make ART (it only stops the generic/garish). This skill invents the bold, web-native artistic MOMENTS that turn the feeling into something you'd screenshot, staying at the ART level — it speaks the web's language (parallax, cinematic scroll, a character that turns to camera, a living background, type-as-image, reveal choreography) but describes what artful thing happens and how it feels, never how to build it (envision speaks pure feeling; stage speaks web-native art like 'a lion's face fills the hero and turns to meet your eyes as you scroll'; realize speaks engineering). Because a model isn't a native artist, it reaches art by inventing several BOLD concepts and choosing the striking-yet-true, STEALING from specific great work and adapting its moves, composing with craft (focal point, scale, negative space, the cinematic moment), and sourcing genuinely artful assets — grounded so it's not generic, restrained so it's not garish (bold != loud), and judged by an OUTSIDE EYE for beauty, never self-certified. Crucially it DECOMPOSES each moment to physical truth — light, shadow, material, colour, depth, form, texture as they really behave — and refuses the lone primitive that only symbolizes a phenomenon (warm light is never just a gradient; depth is never just a box-shadow). Produces the Staging (a bold concept + concrete described moments — each decomposed to physical truth and staged across form factors, mobile-first — + a visual system), confirmed at low fidelity before building (routing to references: the web as an art medium; inventing the concept; decompose to physical truth — the painter's physics; composition & cinematic staging incl. staging across form factors; stealing from great work; grounding, restraint & the taste gate). realize-the-vision: the craftsman that turns a Vision AND a confirmed Staging into a real, running interface by RESEARCHING the truest, soundest means before writing any code — surveys the field (GSAP, Motion, three.js/R3F/angular-three, Web Animations, CSS scroll-driven animations, View Transitions, Lottie/Rive, Canvas/SVG/WebGL, Web Audio, haptics) and its caveats (jank, layout thrash, reduced-motion, accessibility, mobile), knows when to build in code vs. when to source an asset (figurative/photoreal art is generated, licensed, or downloaded — never hand-coded into path-soup — with licensing, review, and art-direction), requires a confirmed Staging before building (else invokes stage-the-vision first), engineers each staged moment, never self-certifies aesthetics, designs and confirms the architecture before building (architecture-first), and verifies against the feeling and the Staging in the running app — routing to a reference library of technique clusters.
